title: Mala Tinero
content:
{{Top|fga|canon=Mala (Wedge Antilles)}} {{Otheruses|Mala}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:Mala.jpg]] |name=Mala Tinero |homeworld=[[Gus Talon/Legends|Gus Talon]]{{Tales|23|Lucky (comic story)}} |birth= |death=[[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], Gus Talon{{CSWECite|II|278|Mala}} |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Dark brown |skin=Fair |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Corellian Resistance/Legends|Corellian Resistance]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I'm going to help him, Wedge. I need to do ''something''.|Tinero, on her father's support of the Rebel Alliance|}} '''Mala Tinero''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who lived on [[Gus Talon/Legends|Gus Talon]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. An apt [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]], she lived and worked with her [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Rallo]], who was a clandestine supporter of the [[Corellian Resistance/Legends|Rebellion]]. Tinero was romantically involved with a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] named [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], whom she attempted to persuade to participate in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|rebellion]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]; however, the pilot was reluctant to get involved. While Antilles was away from Gus Talon on a supply run, Rallo's Rebel ties were discovered by the Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Turrant]], and after Rallo and his men [[Death/Legends|killed]] several of Turrant's [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] in a [[Lightfight|firefight]], the Captain ordered an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the moon. The [[Bombing of Gus Talon|bombardment]] left both Tinero and her father dead. Antilles received lasting emotional scars, but Tinero's death simultaneously acted as a catalyst in his decision to ultimately become involved with the Rebellion. ==Biography== {{Quote|I'll be gone a day or two. You okay with that?''"
"''Wedge… when you come back, will you think about helping Dad? The Rebellion could really use you and your ship.''"
"''I admire what your father is doing. I do. But…''"
"''You just don't see what it has to do with you…|Wedge Antilles and Tinero|}} Mala Tinero''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who lived with her [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Rallo]], on the [[Corellia/Legends|Corellian]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Gus Talon/Legends|Gus Talon]] in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. Rallo was a [[Technician/Legends|mechanic]] who operated a [[Rallo's workshop|workshop]] on the moon while secretly supporting a [[Rebel cell (Gus Talon)|Rebel cell]]; Tinero helped him around the workshop and hoped to become more active herself in the fledgling [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|rebellion]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. In 2 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], she was romantically involved with [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], a young [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] who was operating from Gus Talon as a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. Although Antilles was nervous around Tinero's father, Rallo in fact approved of the boy and helped him perform maintenance on his [[Wedge Antilles's freighter|ship]]. [[File:RalloProtectsMala-SWTales23.png|thumb|left|225px|Mala Tinero flees from Imperial stormtroopers.]] One [[Standard day/Legends|day]], when Tinero and Antilles were at a street market together in [[Gus Talon settlement|one]] of the moon's [[City/Legends|cities]], she began to chase him through the market after he playfully stole her [[Necklace/Legends|necklace]]. When Tinero attempted to leap over a [[Unidentified street vendor|glass vendor's]] stand in her pursuit, she crashed to the ground and brought several of the [[Merchant/Legends|vendor's]] [[Glass/Legends|glass]] sculptures with her. She eventually caught up with Antilles, but he was standing next to Rallo and facing down a pair of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]—Tinero's father had stopped the boy from running straight into the [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] and their [[Captain/Legends|captain]], [[Turrant]], whose patrol was temporarily stationed on the moon. Later, when Antilles was tinkering with his [[Starship/Legends|ship]] and preparing for a job to deliver machine parts out of the [[Corellian system/Legends|system]], Tinero asked him to consider joining their cause. Antilles was reluctant to do so, but he promised that they would further discuss the offer upon his return. Antilles was gone for two days, and during that [[Time/Legends|time]], Tinero and her father were working on a [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] in Rallo's workshop when Captain Turrant arrived and arrested them both on the charge of [[Treason/Legends|collusion]] with the Rebellion. When he threatened to take Tinero away from her father, Rallo drew a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and shot a member of Turrant's stormtrooper [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]]. Tinero fled at a word from her father, tossing a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] to one of his [[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]] employees on her way out. Rallo and his men [[Death/Legends|killed]] the stormtroopers during the [[Lightfight|firefight]], but Turrant escaped and [[Bombing of Gus Talon|ordered]] a retaliatory [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] of the moon. Tinero and Rallo were two of the bombardment's casualties. When Antilles returned to Gus Talon, he found no trace of Tinero, and her death caused a deep hatred of the Empire to blossom within him. He threw in with the Rebellion and, over the [[Standard year/Legends|years]], became one of the foremost heroes of the Alliance and its successor state, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], in their long and ultimately successful war with the Empire.''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' Antilles continued to feel pain and loss over Tinero for years after her death. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|For you, I'll fly like the wind.''"
"''Ooh. Cheesy.|Antilles and Tinero|}} [[File:Flylikethewind.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tinero and Wedge Antilles]] Tinero had a close relationship with her father and at times assisted him with his duties around the workshop. Following in Rallo's footsteps, she demonstrated aptitude as a mechanic. Rallo was himself protective of Tinero; however, he also trusted her with the knowledge of his involvement with the Rebel Alliance. Tinero was resentful of the Empire and was eager to aid the Alliance herself, as well as to recruit Wedge Antilles to the rebel cause. She and Antilles loved each other, although neither of them had the opportunity to tell the other before her death. The two were emotionally very close yet playful with one another, and Tinero was not above poking fun at him for delivering cliched romantic lines. Tinero's death had a deep impact on Antilles, who bore emotional scars for years and became inspired to dedicate his life to fighting the Empire. Tinero had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that fell down to her chin, as well as dark brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and fair skin. ==Equipment== The last time Antilles and Tinero saw each other, shortly before the former left Gus Talon for a job, Tinero wore cyan [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] with yellow trim, along with a golden necklace and [[Bracelet/Legends|bracelet]] and dark [[Boot/Legends|boots]]. While she was working in the workshop at the time of the bombing of her hometown, Tinero had a green suit with a [[Belt/Legends|belt]] and [[Glove/Legends|gloves]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|THE CURSE of WEDGE ANTILLES|Cover of ''Star Wars Tales 23'', referring to the story of "Lucky"|[[Star Wars Tales 23|''Star Wars Tales'' 23]]}} The character of Mala was introduced in "[[Lucky (comic story)|Lucky]]," a short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story written by [[Rob Williams]] and penciled by [[Michel Lacombe]]. It was included in the [[Star Wars Tales 23|twenty-third issue]] of ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'', which was published in [[May]] [[2005]]. Advertised on the cover of the issue as "The curse of Wedge Antilles," "Lucky" tells the story of Mala's death, which inspires Antilles to join the Rebel Alliance but continues to haunt him for years. The [[2012]] reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', which was written by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Paul R. Urquhart]], later provided her last name of Tinero. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Mala Tinero}} *[[Star Wars (2013) 10|''Star Wars'' (2013) 10]] {{Mo}} *{{Tales|23|Lucky (comic story)}} {{1st}} {{Flash}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' {{1stID|Tinero}} *{{InsiderCite|148|Star Wars: Revenge of the Quiz|''Star Wars'': Revenge of the Quiz}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Mala Tinero }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tinero, Mala}} [[Category:Alliance to Restore the Republic members]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Mechanics]]
